mnemonics.title: Forward Chaining Process
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'MSE': Match rules to Working Memory → Select rule using Conflict Resolution → Execute (fire) rule to add new facts. Repeat until goal or no rules fire.

mnemonics.title: Conflict Resolution Strategies
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'RASP': Recency (recent facts first) → Arbitrary (defined order) → Specificity (more conditions first) → Priority (user-defined).

mnemonics.title: Forward vs Backward Chaining
mnemonics.content: Forward: Facts → Conclusions (Bottom-up/D Data-driven). Backward: Goal → Facts (Top-down/Goal-driven). F2C = Facts to Conclusions helps remember Forward Chain.

acronyms.expansion: Left-Hand Side (Condition part of IF-THEN rule)
acronyms.usage: The LHS contains conditions that must be satisfied for the rule to fire.

acronyms.expansion: Right-Hand Side (Action part of IF-THEN rule)
acronyms.usage: The RHS contains actions, typically adding new facts to working memory.
Stores all known facts and inferred facts during inference.
The broader field to which forward chaining belongs.
A popular expert system tool that implements forward chaining.

visualTips.visualization: Draw a cycle: Working Memory → Rule Matcher → Conflict Resolver → Rule Executor → Working Memory (updated). This creates a circular flow showing how facts accumulate.
Think of dominoes falling: One rule fires → adds fact → enables next rule → fires → adds more facts → continues like domino chain reaction.
Left-to-right arrow for Forward (data→goal). Right-to-left arrow for Backward (goal←data). Forward moves in natural reading direction.

visualTips.visualize: Rule as a bridge: Facts on left bank → Rule (bridge) → New facts on right bank. Multiple bridges can connect to create a network/path.

analogies.analogy: Like a detective starting with evidence at a crime scene and applying deduction rules to find suspects - using what is known to find what might be true.
Like a detective's notebook where all observed clues and deduced information are written down for reference.
Like multiple witnesses claiming to have seen the event - the detective must decide which lead to follow first based on reliability, detail, or recency.
Like a light switch: When all conditions (switches) are ON (satisfied), the light (conclusion) turns ON (fires).
